volumeTitle: Stichus. Trinummus. Truculentus. Tale of a Travelling Bag. Fragments
label: Trinummus (Section 2.2.369b - Section 2.4.438b)

translation.label: Scaife English translation
translation.citation: Trinummus: The Three Pieces of Money

sections.label: Section 2.2.369b
sections.translation: Nothing at all , father .

sections.label: Section 2.2.370
sections.original: tu modo ne me prohibeas accipere , si quid det mihi .
sections.translation: Do you only not hinder me from accepting it if he should give anything to me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.371
sections.original: An eo egestatem ei tolerabis , si quid ab illo acceperis ?
sections.translation: And will you be relieving his poverty by that , if you shall accept anything of him ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.372
sections.translation: By that very means , my father .

sections.label: Section 2.2.372b
sections.original: Pol ego istam volo me rationem edoceas .
sections.translation: Faith , I wish that you would instruct me in that method .

sections.label: Section 2.2.372c
sections.translation: Certainly . Do you know of what family he is born ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.373b
sections.translation: I know — of an extremely honourable one .

sections.label: Section 2.2.374
sections.original: Soror illi est adulta virgo grandis : eam cupio , pater ,
sections.translation: He has a sister — a fine young woman now grown up : I wish , father ,

sections.label: Section 2.2.375
sections.translation: to take her without a portion for my wife .

sections.label: Section 2.2.375b
sections.translation: A wife without a portion ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.375c
sections.translation: Just so — your riches saved as well . By these means you will be conferring an extreme favour on him , and in no way could you help him to greater advantage .

sections.label: Section 2.2.378
sections.original: Egone indotatam te uxorem ut patiar ?
sections.translation: Am I to suffer you to take a wife without a portion ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.378b
sections.translation: You must suffer it , father ; and by these means you will be giving an estimable character to our family .

sections.label: Section 2.2.380
sections.original: Multa ego possum docta dicta et quamvis facunde loqui ,
sections.translation: I could give utterance to many a learned saying , and very fluently too : this old age of mine retains stories of old and ancient times . But , since I see that you are courting friendship and esteem for our family , although I have been opposed to you , I thus give my decision — I will permit you ; ask for the girl , and marry her .

sections.label: Section 2.2.384b
sections.translation: May the Gods preserve you to me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.385
sections.original: sed adde ad istam gratiam unum .
sections.translation: But , to this favour add one thing .

sections.label: Section 2.2.385b
sections.translation: But what is this one thing ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.385c
sections.translation: I will tell you . Do you go to him , do you solicit him , and do you ask for her yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.2.386b
sections.translation: Think of that now .

sections.label: Section 2.2.387
sections.original: Nimio citius transiges : firmum omne erit quod tu egeris .
sections.translation: You will transact it much more speedily : all will be made sure of that you do . One word of yours in this matter will be of more consequence than a hundred of mine .

sections.label: Section 2.2.389
sections.original: Ecce autem in benignitate hoc repperi , negotium .
sections.translation: See , now , how , in my kindness , I have undertaken this matter .

sections.label: Section 2.2.390
sections.translation: My assistance shall be given .

sections.label: Section 2.2.390b
sections.original: Lepidus vivis . haec sunt aedes , hic habet ;
sections.translation: You really are a kind father . This is the house here he dwells . ( He points to the house of CHARMIDES .) Lesbonicus is his name . Mind and attend to the business ; I will await you at home .

sections.label: Section 2.3.392
sections.original: Non optuma haec sunt , neque ut ego aequom censeo ;
sections.translation: These things are not for the best , nor as I think they ought to be ; but still , they are better than that which is downright bad . But this one circumstance consoles myself and my thoughts-namely ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.395
sections.original: quia qui nihil aliud nisi quod sibi soli placet
sections.translation: that he who counsels in respect to a son nothing else but that which pleases himself alone , only plays the fool ; he becomes wretched in mind , and yet he is no nearer bringing it about . He is preparing a very inclement winter for his own old age when he arouses that unseasonable storm .

sections.label: Section 2.3.400
sections.original: sed aperiuntur aedes quo ibam : commodum
sections.translation: ( The door of the house of CHARMIDES opens .) But the house is opened to which I was going ; most conveniently , Lesbonicus himself is coming out of doors with his servant .

sections.label: Section 2.4.402
sections.original: Minus quíndecim dies sunt , quom pro hisce aedibus
sections.translation: ’ Tis less than fifteen days since you received from Callicles forty minae for this house ; is it not as I say , Stasimus ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.404b
sections.translation: When I consider ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.405
sections.translation: I think I remember that it was so .

sections.label: Section 2.4.405b
sections.translation: What has been done with it ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.406
sections.original: Comessum , expotum ; exussum : elotum in balineis ,
sections.translation: It has been eaten and drunk up — spent away in unguents , washed away in baths [*] . The fishmonger and the baker have carried it off : butchers , too , and cooks , green-grocers , perfumers , and poulterers ; ’ twas quickly consumed . I ’ faith ! that money was made away with not less speedily

sections.label: Section 2.4.410
sections.original: quam si tu obicias formicis papaverem .
sections.translation: than if you were to throw a poppy among the ants .

sections.label: Section 2.4.411
sections.original: Minus hercle in istis rebus sumptumst sex minis .
sections.translation: By my troth , less has been spent on those items than six minae ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.412
sections.translation: Besides , what have you given to your mistresses ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.412b
sections.translation: That I am including as well in it .

sections.label: Section 2.4.413
sections.translation: Besides , what have I pilfered of it ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.413b
sections.translation: Aye , that item is a very heavy one .

sections.label: Section 2.4.414
sections.original: Non tibi illud apparere , si sumas , potest ;
sections.translation: That cannot so appear to you , if you make all due deductions [*] ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.415
sections.original: nisi tu immortale rere esse argentum tibi .
sections.translation: unless you think that your money is everlasting .

sections.label: Section 2.4.416
sections.original: Sero atque stulte , prius quod cautum oportuit ,
sections.translation: ( Aside .) Too late and unwisely ,— a caution that should have been used before ,— after he has devoured his substance , he reckons up the account too late .

sections.label: Section 2.4.418
sections.translation: The account , however , of this money is by no means clear .

sections.label: Section 2.4.419
sections.original: Ratio quidem hercle apparet : argentum οἴχεται .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , the account is very clear : the money ’ s gone [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.4.420
sections.translation: Did you not receive forty minae from Callicles , and did he not receive from you the house in possession ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.421b
sections.translation: Very good .

sections.label: Section 2.4.422
sections.original: Pol opino adfinis noster aedis vendidit ;
sections.translation: Troth , I think our neighbour has sold his house [*] . When his father shall come from abroad , his place is in the beggar ’ s gate [*] , unless , perchance , he should creep into his son ’ s stomach [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.4.425
sections.translation: There were a thousand Olympic drachmae [*] paid to the banker [*] , which you were owing upon account .

sections.label: Section 2.4.427
sections.translation: Those , I suppose , that I was security for [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.427b
sections.translation: Say , rather [*] , Those that I paid down — for that young man whom you used to say [*] was so rich .

sections.label: Section 2.4.429
sections.translation: It was so done .

sections.label: Section 2.4.429b
sections.translation: Yes , just to be squandered away .

sections.label: Section 2.4.429c
sections.translation: That was done as well .

sections.label: Section 2.4.430
sections.original: nam nunc eum vidi miserum et me eius miseritumst .
sections.translation: But I saw him in a pitiable state , and I did have pity on him .

sections.label: Section 2.4.431
sections.original: Miseret te aliorum , tui nec miseret nec pudet .
sections.translation: You have pity on others , and you have neither pity nor shame for yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.4.432
sections.translation: ’ Tis time to accost him .

sections.label: Section 2.4.432b
sections.translation: Is this Philto that is coming here ? Troth , ’ tis he himself .

sections.label: Section 2.4.433b
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I could wish he was my slave , together with his savings [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.4.435
sections.original: Erum atque servom plurumum Philto iubet
sections.translation: Philto right heartily wishes health to both master and servant , Lesbonicus and Stasimus .

sections.label: Section 2.4.436b
sections.translation: May the Gods give you , Philto , whatever you may wish for . How is your son ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.438
sections.translation: He wishes well to you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.438b
sections.translation: In good sooth , he does for me what I do for him in return !
